metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the eval_solver code-review arm protocol: valid prompt_id strings, required case fields (diff/changed_files/ticket_id), fire/no-fire determination rule for multi-overlay output, and error fallback, so AC #1 is fully specified.
findings.finding: The plan's AC #1 references an 'eval_solver code-review arm' as a named sub-step that must exist before other stories can gate against it, but eval_solver.run_case today handles only completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment, and plan-review criterion arms. No code-review arm exists in the codebase and no eval spec (code-review*.eval.yaml) is present. The plan never defines the protocol for this arm: which prompt_id strings are valid, what case fields are required (diff? ticket_id? changed_files?), how fire/no-fire is determined for a multi-overlay multi-pass gate, or what the fallback is if the gate run itself errors.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #1: 'Eval foundation (incl. an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers) exists and gates the rest.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan section (or story note) specifying: the arm dispatches on prompt_ids like 'code-review-base', 'code-review-<overlay>'; case fields include `diff` (patch text), `changed_files` (list), optional `repo_files` (fixture); fire = non-empty `findings` after Pass-1; the arm runs only Pass-1 (not the full four-pass gate) over a disposable fixture repo; error fallback = raise ValueError as per existing pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the mechanism for 'content-triggered overlays' in AC item 4: clarify whether (a) the trigger is a new deterministic diff-content scan (describe how it plugs into overlay_union as a third input alongside glob and recommend), (b) it relies on base-reviewer escalation (the existing recommend prong — if so, rename to avoid implying a new capability), or (c) it is a new applies_to glob class — so the implementer knows which codepath to extend and eval cases can test the actual trigger condition.
findings.finding: AC item 4 names 'content-triggered overlay capability' as a distinct new mechanism, but the plan does not specify how this third trigger type integrates with the existing two-prong overlay_union formula (glob ∪ LLM-recommend). The ambiguity leaves the implementer to choose between adding a new deterministic diff-content scan codepath (significant blast-radius: new trigger prong in overlay_union, new registry slot) or simply adding new overlay ids and relying on base-reviewer escalation (the recommend prong, which already exists). These are architecturally divergent implementations.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC item 4: 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC 4 to: 'deletion-impact and scope-intent overlay ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS; trigger condition specified as [glob-match on deletion-heavy diffs / base-reviewer escalation / deterministic diff-stat threshold] and wired into overlay_union [as a new input / via the recommend prong / via applies_to globs].' Pick exactly one mechanism and name it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC to f93a (or 1669) to run the eval corpus against the pre-change gate and persist findings/run, nit-rate, and FP/verify-drop-rate as a named baseline artifact before any prompt change lands.
findings.finding: No child explicitly owns recording a quantitative pre-change baseline artifact (findings/run, nit-rate, FP/verify-drop rate over the full eval corpus). The parent Success Criteria require 'story A records the current (pre-change) gate's findings/run, nit-rate, and FP/verify-drop rate over the eval corpus as the baseline'; f93a encodes one experiment's no-fire/recall eval case as baseline but does not produce or persist the numeric run-level metrics; 1669 emits metrics going forward but not a pre-change snapshot.
findings.location: child f93a-b947-558b-469c
findings.suggested_fix: Extend f93a AC2 to read: 'A baseline run of the full eval corpus is executed against the pre-change gate and its findings/run, nit-rate, and verify-drop rate are committed as a baseline artifact (e.g., evals/baseline_metrics.json); subsequent stories must beat these numbers.'

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align AC2 with the full scope implied by 'story A' in Success Criteria: explicitly state that the baseline records findings/run, nit-rate, AND FP/verify-drop rate over the eval corpus (or rename/remove the 'story A' reference to avoid implying a decomposition that doesn't exist).
findings.finding: 'Story A' is referenced in Success Criteria as a named decomposition unit ('story A records the current (pre-change) gate's findings/run'), but the plan contains no story decomposition section and no AC is labeled 'story A'. An implementer reading AC2 ('A pre-change baseline is recorded') cannot determine whether it fully covers what 'story A' entails or whether story A has additional scope.
findings.location: Success Criteria (paragraph 2) vs Acceptance Criteria item 2
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'story A records the current (pre-change) gate's findings/run, nit-rate, and FP/verify-drop rate over the eval corpus as the baseline' in Success Criteria with 'AC2 records the current gate's findings/run, nit-rate, and FP/verify-drop rate over the eval corpus as the baseline', and expand AC2 to enumerate all three metrics explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring an ADR or CLAUDE.md section that captures the governing principle (domain-specific recall in overlays, generic Pass-2) and the content-triggered overlay contract, so the architectural decision survives beyond this session.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — the governing principle that 'domain-specific recall AND FP-guards live in each specialist overlay; the generic Pass-2 verifier is never taught a single domain's rubric' — and a new content-triggered overlay capability registered in OVERLAY_IDS, but no AC calls for capturing this design decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update. Future maintainers have no durable artifact explaining why the overlay/verifier contract is structured this way.
findings.location: Context (governing principle paragraph) and AC: 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Governing principle and content-triggered overlay contract documented in CLAUDE.md or an ADR (e.g., docs/adr/0NNN-overlay-verifier-contract.md), referenced from OVERLAY_IDS registry.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Quote (or link to a pinned source) the exact experiment-backed linter-deference line that must appear in the base prompt, so the AC is self-contained and verifiable without external lookup.
findings.finding: The 'experiment-backed positive linter-deference line' is not quoted or defined anywhere in the plan; an executor cannot write or verify the base prompt without knowing the actual text of this line.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Base prompt carries the experiment-backed positive linter-deference line only.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single quoted sentence to this AC, e.g.: 'Base prompt carries exactly this line: "Defer to passing linter/formatter output unless the rule is demonstrably inapplicable to this diff" (validated by FP-reduction experiment <link/commit>).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each of the five specialist overlays, state the minimum observable enrichment (e.g., specific checklist items drawn from dso's rubric, named FP guards to add, or a concrete before/after diff excerpt) so the AC is evaluable in-session.
findings.finding: 'All five specialist overlays enriched' is effort-framed and outcome-vague; 'enriched' sets no observable, verifiable bar — an executor cannot determine when any individual overlay is done.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'All five specialist overlays (security, performance, api-compat, db-migrations, llm-prompts) enriched.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name or quote the two halves of the behavioral-testing standard explicitly in the AC (e.g., 'half 1: test-exists check; half 2: behavior-not-implementation assertion rule') so the requirement is self-contained.
findings.finding: 'Tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves)' uses 'both halves' as an undefined reference; an executor cannot identify what the two halves are without external context.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves); Pass 2 stays generic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'both halves' of the behavioral-testing standard in the AC (or in a referenced document) so the criterion is independently verifiable.
findings.finding: The AC for the tests overlay references 'both halves' of the behavioral-testing standard without defining what those halves are anywhere in the plan. No other section names or describes these two halves.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 5: 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for prompt and overlay changes (e.g., version-tag the pre-change prompts in the repo, document the revert command, or specify an expand-contract release sequence) so that a production regression can be reversed without an emergency manual hunt.
findings.finding: The plan modifies the Pass-2 verifier prompt, base prompt, and all five specialist overlays — components that existing rebar users rely on for consistent gate behavior — but states no rollback or back-out plan if production behavior degrades beyond the eval corpus.
findings.location: Success Criteria / AC list (Pass-2 verifier upgrade, base prompt, overlays)
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan, add a 'Rollback path' section: (1) tag the current prompt files in version control before any story lands (e.g., git tag pre-dso-calibration); (2) document that reverting to the tag restores prior gate behavior; (3) specify that Story A's baseline eval run is re-run against the tag to confirm the rollback is clean. This costs near-zero effort and satisfies the T4 remedy requirement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Sharpen 'enriched' and 'landed' ACs with observable completion criteria (e.g., 'each specialist overlay contains at least N dso-sourced FP-guard rules verified by the eval corpus' and 'FP-ledger is a committed file at path X with schema Y').
findings.finding: Several acceptance criteria use verbs ('enriched', 'landed') without specifying what observable end-state constitutes completion, making it impossible to deterministically verify these ACs against the measurable-improvement success criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'All five specialist overlays enriched' and 'FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, coach moves, approach-viability note landed'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify 'content-triggered overlay capability' as a distinct mechanism vs. enriching base-reviewer escalation instructions (Round-B) for deletion-impact and scope-intent cases, or explicitly define what 'content-triggered' means in terms of the existing overlay_union dispatch.
findings.finding: The plan introduces 'content-triggered overlay capability' as a new trigger modality (AC line 4) but does not justify it against the two existing trigger mechanisms (Round-A glob-triggers on `applies_to` file patterns; Round-B base-reviewer LLM escalation). Deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays are cited as users of this new modality, but it is unclear why enriching base-reviewer escalation instructions (the existing Round-B path) or extending `applies_to` glob patterns cannot cover these cases.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria line 4: 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'prefer AST-LSP' in the Pass-2 verifier upgrade means (a) prompt wording only (no tooling change needed) or (b) a new AST/LSP tool integration; if (b), name the library/approach and resolve the capability assertion before committing.
findings.finding: The Pass-2 verifier upgrade AC states 'prefer AST-LSP' as a verification strategy, but no AST or LSP tooling is present in the codebase (the verifier is an agentic step using only read_file tools), and no new tool dependency or integration is named. This is an unverified capability assertion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria line 6: 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP).'
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is prompt-wording only (instruct the verifier to cite specific file:line references and reason about structure rather than pattern-matching), say so explicitly in the AC. If a new tool integration is intended, add a spike story to evaluate tree-sitter or pyright LSP as a read-only tool and record the decision in an ADR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval_solver code-review arm's interface: the case schema it consumes (diff text, changed-file list, or fixture-repo patch), the workflow entry-point it calls (e.g. `batch_runner` or a slimmed single-overlay path), and whether it reuses `case_store` or a new diff-seeded fixture scaffold — so the first AC item is implementable without mid-sprint architectural discovery.
findings.finding: The plan's first (gating) AC asserts 'an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers exists and gates the rest', but eval_solver.run_case currently has no code-review dispatch path — only three fixed agentic ops and the plan-review criterion arm — and the plan does not specify how the arm will be wired: what case schema it accepts (diff content, changed files, PR ref?), which workflow entry-point it invokes, or whether the disposable git scaffold applies.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet: 'Eval foundation (incl. an eval_solver code-review arm + gating scorers) exists and gates the rest.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'both halves' of the behavioral-testing standard explicitly in the plan (e.g., half-1 = structural coverage check 'does the change add tests?'; half-2 = behavioral assertion quality check 'do the tests assert behavior not just call the code?') so the tests overlay AC is verifiable.
findings.finding: AC #5 says the tests overlay carries the 'full behavioral-testing standard (both halves)' but neither the plan nor the current code-review-tests.md defines what 'both halves' means. An executor enriching the overlay cannot determine what two-part structure to implement.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #5: 'tests overlay carries the full behavioral-testing standard (both halves); Pass 2 stays generic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the instruction text and decision rule for each of the four named Pass-2 verifier modes (contradiction, caller, arithmetic, absence) and state the fallback behavior when AST/LSP tools are unavailable.
findings.finding: AC #6 names four specific Pass-2 verifier upgrade modes (contradiction, caller, arithmetic, absence) and an 'AST-LSP preference' directive, but the plan never specifies the instruction text, the decision rule for each mode, or the fallback when AST/LSP tooling is unavailable. The current code-review-verify.md contains none of these modes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #6: 'Pass-2 verifier prompt upgraded (contradiction/caller/arithmetic/absence; prefer AST-LSP).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the grounding-effort and health telemetry fields: their names, types, which schema object carries them, and the pass/step where each is computed and written.
findings.finding: AC #10 names 'grounding-effort/health telemetry' as a deliverable but never defines: what fields constitute this telemetry, which schema carries them (the base output schema, overlay output schema, or a separate telemetry record), or how/where the counter or state is incremented during a review run.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #10: 'FP-ledger convention, grounding-effort/health telemetry, coach moves, approach-viability note landed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the scope-intent overlay's trailer-resolution protocol: how trailer references are parsed, which tool calls fetch each ticket, how the union of their AC/scope is assembled, and what the fallback is for an inaccessible or missing referenced ticket.
findings.finding: AC #9 says the scope-intent overlay 'checks the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets,' but no protocol is defined for how the overlay resolves trailer references to ticket content, what tools it calls (show_ticket? list_tickets?), what 'UNION' means operationally, or what the fallback is when a referenced ticket is missing or inaccessible.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #9: 'scope-intent overlay checks the diff against the UNION of all trailer-referenced tickets.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the content-triggered overlay capability: the trigger predicate schema (what content signals fire it), how it differs from glob-triggers, and which specific content patterns trigger deletion-impact and scope-intent respectively.
findings.finding: AC #4 references two new overlay IDs ('deletion-impact' and 'scope-intent') that must be registered in OVERLAY_IDS, but neither exists in the current OVERLAY_IDS tuple and neither has a defined content-trigger predicate. The plan names them as needing a 'content-triggered overlay capability' but never defines what content triggers each one.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #4: 'Content-triggered overlay capability shipped (new ids registered in OVERLAY_IDS); deletion-impact and scope-intent overlays use it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit 'coordinate on registry.py / OVERLAY_IDS' note in both f983 and 3c5d scopes (mirroring the criteria_routing.json coordination note), and declare a merge serialization order.
findings.finding: registry.py (OVERLAY_IDS tuple) is edited by both f983-9441-35f4-445f (adds `deletion-impact`) and 3c5d-b21a-9af2-470a (adds `scope-intent`), but only criteria_routing.json is called out as a SHARED/coordinate file; the registry.py merge conflict risk is not acknowledged by either child, creating an unguarded ordering/merge gap.
findings.location: child 3c5d-b21a-9af2-470a
findings.suggested_fix: Add to both children's Scope sections: 'registry.py (OVERLAY_IDS — SHARED with [sibling]; serialize this edit or use a single additive commit that appends both ids together).' Designate one story as the owner of the base OVERLAY_IDS edit and the other as additive on top.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit 'depends on f93a (eval foundation)' prerequisite note to each child that has an eval AC (da8a, da25, 4b56, f983, 3c5d, 1669).
findings.finding: No child explicitly declares a sequencing dependency on f93a as a prerequisite, yet several children (da8a, da25, 4b56, f983, 3c5d, 7c58, 1669) each include an eval AC that requires the f93a harness and scorers to already exist. If any child merges before f93a, its eval AC cannot be satisfied, violating the parent AC that says the eval foundation 'gates the rest'.
findings.location: child da8a-75f5-ccb2-4b5f
findings.suggested_fix: In each dependent child's Scope or Why section, add: 'Prerequisite: f93a eval foundation must be merged first; this story's eval ACs cannot pass without it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to 1669 confirming the approach-viability note is emitted as an additive, optional field with no breaking schema change, and that existing consumers (including plan-review paths) are unaffected.
findings.finding: The approach-viability advisory note (child 1669) adds a new field to the verdict assembly output (post-Pass-3 signal), but no child addresses backward compatibility for existing downstream consumers of the verdict object that may not expect the new field.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 1669 AC3: 'The advisory note is emitted as an optional/nullable field in the existing verdict envelope; the verdict schema change is additive and backward-compatible; plan-review verdict consumers are unaffected (verified by plan-review evals remaining green).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
